expanded_desc: 389 Directory Server access/audit log - bind, search, modify, delete operations on the LDAP directory. Authentication and Identity_Management data model contributor on Red Hat IdM hosts.
Generic web access log sourcetype - default for HTTP servers when no vendor-specific routing exists. Should be re-routed to apache:access / iis / nginx etc. for proper CIM mapping.
NCSA combined log format access log - the classic Apache CombinedLogFormat (with referer and user-agent). Feeds the Web data model.
NCSA combined log format extended with a cookie field - a Splunk-prebuilt variant of the Apache combined format including Cookie header. Feeds the Web data model.
Generic web/SSL access log catch-all - should be routed to apache:access / nginx:access / iis as appropriate. Feeds the Web data model when properly typed.
Active Directory logs collected from domain controllers - covers directory service, replication, and authentication events depending on auditing scope.
Apache ActiveMQ message broker log - broker startup, queue/topic activity, connection events. Operational messaging telemetry.
Active Directory Federation Services admin event log - service configuration changes, certificate updates, relying-party trust modifications.
Active Directory Federation Services audit event log - federated authentication outcomes (success/failure), token issuance, claim rules evaluated.
Azure VM agent console output captured during extension execution - stdout/stderr from extension handler scripts.
Azure VM extension agent launcher log - extension handler process startup events.
AIDE (Advanced Intrusion Detection Environment) report log - filesystem integrity scan diff output. Feeds the Change data model for Linux baseline drift.
VMware Workspace ONE UEM (formerly AirWatch) management events - device enrollment, policy state, compliance posture. MDM-grade telemetry.
Akamai Connector for SIEM JSON output - WAF/Kona/Bot Manager events delivered via the Cloud Monitor connector. Feeds the Web and IDS data models.
Akamai SIEM connector event stream - WAF and bot-management security events delivered via the SIEM Integration API.
Concatenated form of akamai:siem produced by older add-on versions - same Akamai WAF/Bot Manager event payload.
Linux /etc/alternatives update-alternatives logs - tracks symbolic-link based switches between equivalent packages (e.g., default JDK, default editor). Low-value operational telemetry.
AWS CloudWatch agent operational and diagnostic logs from EC2 or on-premises servers - agent startup, metric/log collection cycles, configuration reloads, and errors.
Amazon ECS task and service lifecycle events (start, stop, fail, scale, deployment) emitted via EventBridge or the ECS event stream.
AWS SSM agent operational log written on managed instances - agent startup, command and association processing, plugin execution, and communication errors with the SSM service.
Ansible automation log - playbook run results, task status, host facts. Operational with Change data model overlap when configuration changes are applied.
Generic Apache HTTPD log catch-all - typically pre-dates the apache:access/apache:error split. Prefer the specific subtypes for parsed extractions.
Apache HTTPD access log (parsed) - per-request method, URI, status, bytes, user-agent, referer, client IP. Primary Web data model source for Apache deployments.
Apache HTTPD error log (parsed) - module-level errors, configuration warnings, mod_security alerts, and process failures.
Underscore-named variant of apache:error - same parsed content; choice depends on add-on version installed.
Kubernetes API server log - controller manager and kube-apiserver process logs from /var/log/kubernetes or container stdout. Operational/diagnostic core control-plane source.
Auxiliary agent log lines from an RDS instance, forwarded via the RDS log groups (e.g., backup agent, monitoring agent, or vendor extension agents).
RDS database engine error log: connection failures, query errors, restart and crash messages, replication issues.
RDS PostgreSQL engine log: client connections, query errors, autovacuum activity, checkpoint events, and slow queries.
RDS service-level instance events emitted by the RDS control plane: failovers, scheduled backups, parameter group changes, maintenance events.
AppArmor LSM log - mandatory access control profile enforcement on Debian/Ubuntu/SUSE. Records ALLOWED, DENIED, and AUDIT events for confined applications.
Ubuntu crash report daemon logs from /var/log/apport.log - records application crashes captured for diagnostic upload. Operational debugging data, minimal security value.
Generic application service alert/event sourcetype - vendor varies. CIM mapping depends on payload.
Debian/Ubuntu APT package manager logs from /var/log/apt/history.log and term.log. Records install/upgrade/remove operations, useful for software inventory and change tracking.
APT command history log - the human-readable record of apt invocations independent of the structured apt log. Forensic evidence of admin package operations.
Debian/Ubuntu APT terminal output captured to /var/log/apt/term.log - verbose dpkg invocation transcripts that complement the structured history.log entries.
Argo CD audit log - GitOps continuous delivery events: app sync, rollback, RBAC, and CLI invocations.
JFrog Artifactory access log - per-request artifact download/upload/delete with user, repo, and result. Feeds Web and Change data models.
JFrog Artifactory audit log - admin/security configuration changes, permission, and user lifecycle events.
Aruba AirWave WLAN management log - AP, controller, and client events tracked by AirWave. Operational with NAC overlap.
Aruba ClearPass NAC policy decisions - RADIUS/TACACS authentication, posture, and role assignment. Authentication data model contributor.
Asterisk VoIP PBX system message log - call signaling, registration, channel state, and admin activity messages.
Elastic Auditbeat agent log - host-level audit events from auditd, file integrity, and system module shipped by Auditbeat.
Linux audit daemon raw log from /var/log/audit/audit.log - the underlying source feeding most linux:audit:* parsed sourcetypes via the Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux.
Generic /var/log/auth.log Debian/Ubuntu authentication facility - SSHD, sudo, su, PAM events. Primary Authentication data model source on Debian-family systems.
Avast endpoint antivirus log - scan results, detected malware, real-time protection events. Feeds the Malware data model.
AVEVA Historian (formerly Wonderware) process data tag readings - time-series industrial process variables.
AVG endpoint antivirus log - scan and real-time protection detections. Feeds the Malware data model.
AWS Application Load Balancer access logs delivered to S3 - HTTP request and response details, target group routing decisions, TLS cipher/protocol, and request/target processing times.

AWS CloudTrail API activity logs capturing every authenticated request to the AWS API: identity (IAM user/role/assumed-role), source IP, user agent, request parameters, and response. Core data source for AWS audit and threat detection.
CloudWatch metrics for DynamoDB tables, indexes, and streams: consumed read/write capacity, throttled events, system errors, latency.
CloudWatch metrics for EBS volumes: read/write throughput and IOPS, queue depth, burst balance, idle time, volume status.
CloudWatch metrics for EC2 instances: CPU utilization, network in/out, EBS throughput, status check pass/fail, CPU credit balance for T-family instances.
CloudWatch metrics for ElastiCache (Redis and Memcached) clusters: client connections, CPU/memory utilization, evictions, cache hits and misses, replication lag.
CloudWatch metrics for Classic, Application, and Network Load Balancers: request count, latency, HTTP 4xx/5xx counts, healthy/unhealthy host counts, target connection errors.
AWS CloudWatch Events / EventBridge stream - near-real-time events for AWS service state changes (e.g., EC2 instance state, S3 object created, RDS snapshot complete) and custom application events.
GuardDuty threat findings delivered through CloudWatch Events/EventBridge (vs. the aws:guardduty modular input path).
CloudWatch metrics for Kinesis Data Streams and Firehose: incoming records and bytes, outgoing records and bytes, iterator age, write/read throttle counts.
CloudWatch metrics for Lambda functions: invocation count, error count, duration, throttles, concurrent executions, dead-letter errors.
CloudWatch metrics for RDS database instances and clusters: CPU utilization, database connections, read/write IOPS and latency, replica lag, freeable memory, swap usage.
CloudWatch metrics for S3 buckets (requires request metrics enabled): request counts by operation, bytes downloaded/uploaded, 4xx/5xx error counts, first-byte and total request latency.
CloudWatch metrics for SNS topics: published messages, delivery successes/failures by protocol (HTTP/SQS/Lambda/SMS/email), message size, filter evaluations.
CloudWatch metrics for SQS queues: visible/in-flight/delayed message counts, approximate age of oldest message, sent/received/deleted rates, empty receives.
GuardDuty findings exported through CloudWatch Logs - an alternate delivery path to aws:guardduty for environments that aggregate findings into a CloudWatch log group.
VPC Flow Logs delivered via CloudWatch Logs - per-interface records of accepted/rejected IP traffic: source/destination IP and port, protocol, packets, bytes, TCP flags, action.
AWS Config compliance evaluations against configured rules: resource compliance state changes (COMPLIANT/NON_COMPLIANT), evaluation details, drift detection.

AWS resource description snapshots collected by the Splunk Add-on for AWS: EC2 instances and addresses, IAM users/roles/policies, load balancers, security groups, key pairs, RDS clusters, and many more resource types.
Amazon EKS Kubernetes API server audit logs: API request URI and verb, authenticated identity, response code, and impersonation chain. Used to detect privileged access and policy violations.
Classic Elastic Load Balancer access logs delivered to S3: client IP and port, request line, backend instance, response code, request/response/backend processing times.
Classic Elastic Load Balancer access logs delivered to S3: client IP and port, request line, backend instance, response code, request/response/backend processing times.

Amazon GuardDuty threat findings derived from VPC Flow Logs, CloudTrail, and DNS logs. Finding types include reconnaissance, instance compromise, credential exfiltration, crypto-mining, and policy violations.

Amazon Inspector vulnerability and reachability findings across EC2 instances, ECR container images, and Lambda functions. Each finding includes severity, CVE, affected package, and remediation guidance.
AWS IoT Core MQTT/HTTPS message logs and lifecycle events: device connect/disconnect, publishes, subscribes, rule engine invocations, and authorization decisions.
Amazon Macie findings from S3 data scans: sensitive-data findings (PII, financial info, credentials) and policy findings (public buckets, encryption disabled, replication to untrusted accounts).
AWS Network Firewall alert events generated by Suricata-compatible stateful rules: alert message, signature ID, source/destination addresses, application protocol.
AWS Network Firewall flow logs - per-connection records of traffic inspected by the firewall: 5-tuple, byte and packet counts, action (PASS/DROP/ALERT).
RDS database audit log - SQL statements executed against the engine when an audit plugin (MariaDB Audit, pgAudit, SQL Server Audit) is enabled. Captures connection, DDL, and DML activity.
RDS engine error log: server-level errors, restart events, replication problems, deadlocks, and recovery messages. Distinct from app:rds:error which is a forwarded engine log within the OS log group.
Route 53 DNS query logs delivered to CloudWatch Logs: query name and type, resolver IP, edge location, response code. Used for DNS-based threat hunting and resolver visibility.
S3 server access logs: requester IAM identity or anonymous, bucket and key, operation (REST.GET.OBJECT/PUT.OBJECT/etc.), HTTP status, bytes transferred, request and turnaround time.
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - installed applications and versions on managed instances (EC2, edge, on-prem).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - AWS-provided components installed on managed instances (CloudWatch agent, SSM agent, EC2Launch, etc.).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - billing-related metadata captured on managed instances (license type, billing product codes).
AWS Systems Manager compliance data - per-item compliance results (patch, association, or custom compliance type) for each managed instance.
AWS Systems Manager compliance data - instance-level compliance summary aggregating all compliance types into compliant/non-compliant counts.
AWS Systems Manager inventory exposed by Amazon Inspector - CVE list for installed packages on each managed instance.
AWS Systems Manager inventory exposed by Amazon Inspector - metric data emitted by the package-scanner subsystem (scan duration, packages evaluated).
AWS Systems Manager inventory exposed by Amazon Inspector - third-party package inventory (non-OS packages) on managed instances.
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - detailed OS and hardware information for each managed instance (kernel version, CPU model, instance type).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - summary information for each managed instance (platform, ping status, agent version, association status).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - network configuration of each managed instance (interfaces, IPv4/IPv6 addresses, MAC addresses, default gateways).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - patching status summary per instance (installed/missing/failed patch counts, last operation time).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - resource group memberships for each managed instance.
AWS Systems Manager compliance data - compliance scan results for installed software (patch and configuration compliance).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - installed Windows services on managed instances (name, status, start type).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data - tag metadata applied to each managed instance.
AWS Systems Manager inventory - installed Windows roles and features on managed Windows instances.
AWS Systems Manager inventory - installed Windows updates (KB IDs, install date) on managed Windows instances.

S3 object events delivered via Kinesis Data Firehose where the Splunk modular input could not classify the payload to a more specific sourcetype. Usually indicates a missing or misconfigured event processor mapping.
AWS Security Hub aggregated findings in ASFF (AWS Security Finding Format) from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, IAM Access Analyzer, AWS Config, and integrated partner tools.
AWS Systems Manager compliance summary delivered via the Splunk Add-on for AWS modular input (vs. the S3-staged aws:s3:ssm:compliancesummary path).
AWS Systems Manager per-item compliance delivered via the Splunk Add-on for AWS modular input (vs. the S3-staged aws:s3:ssm:complianceitem path).
AWS Systems Manager detailed instance information delivered via the Splunk Add-on for AWS modular input (vs. the S3-staged variant).
AWS Systems Manager instance summary information delivered via the Splunk Add-on for AWS modular input (vs. the S3-staged variant).
AWS Systems Manager inventory data delivered via the Splunk Add-on for AWS modular input - aggregated view of installed applications, services, and network configuration.
AWS Systems Manager patch summary delivered via the Splunk Add-on for AWS modular input (vs. the S3-staged variant).
AWS WAF (web application firewall) web ACL logs: request URI, headers, source IP, action (ALLOW/BLOCK/COUNT/CAPTCHA), and matching rule. Used for WAF tuning and web-attack analysis.
Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) directory audit logs - admin actions, user/group/application/policy changes, role assignments.
Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) device inventory - registered/joined devices, OS, compliance state, owner.
Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) group inventory - group definitions, membership, group type (security, M365, dynamic).
Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) sign-in logs - interactive and non-interactive authentications including user, app, conditional access result, MFA status, risk level.
Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) user inventory - account attributes, license assignments, account enabled state, last sign-in.

Azure Activity log - base sourcetype before per-category routing. Captures all control-plane operations (admin/read/write/delete), service health, alerts, and recommendations across an Azure subscription.
Azure Activity log - non-CRUD action events against resources (e.g., restart, generalize, scale).
Azure Activity log - administrative operations (resource creation, role assignment, policy assignment, deployment).
Azure Monitor alert events fired from metric, log, or activity log alert rules.
Azure resource diagnostic logs archived via the Activity log pipeline - lower-priority retention stream.
Azure Activity log - delete operations against resources. High-value for change/threat detection.
Azure VPN Gateway and ExpressRoute Gateway diagnostic logs - tunnel status changes, route updates, IKE phase 1/2 negotiations, and BGP peering events.
Azure VPN Gateway IKE phase 1/2 negotiation diagnostic log - useful for troubleshooting site-to-site tunnels.

description: Azure Event Hub Kafka coordinator log
expanded_desc: Azure Event Hubs Kafka surface - coordinator log for consumer group rebalances and partition assignment.

description: Azure Event Hub Kafka user error log
expanded_desc: Azure Event Hubs Kafka surface - user-facing error log for producers/consumers (auth failures, throttling, protocol errors).
Azure Load Balancer health probe events - backend pool member up/down transitions.
Azure NSG rule evaluation events - which rule matched a packet decision (allow/deny).
Azure NSG flow logs - per-flow 5-tuple records of inbound and outbound traffic evaluated by NSG rules.
Azure NSG rule hit counters - aggregated counts of traffic that matched each NSG rule.
Azure Activity log - service operational log entries that aren't admin/read/write but represent internal service events.
Azure VPN Gateway point-to-site (P2S) diagnostic logs - client connections, authentication outcomes (OpenVPN, IKEv2, SSTP), and disconnect events.
Azure Policy evaluation events - compliance state changes, policy assignments, exemption applications.
Azure Activity log - read operations against resources. Useful for data-access audit and reconnaissance detection.
Azure Advisor recommendation events - cost, security, reliability, and performance recommendations.
Azure Resource Health events - per-resource availability changes (Available/Degraded/Unavailable/Unknown).
Azure routing diagnostic log - effective route table changes and next-hop resolution events.
Azure Service Health events - service issues, planned maintenance, health advisories, and security advisories from the Azure platform.
Azure Storage delete operations - blob/file/queue/table delete activity for audit and DLP.
Azure Storage read operations - blob/file/queue/table read activity, including SAS-token usage.
Azure Storage write operations - blob/file/queue/table create and update activity.
Azure VPN tunnel diagnostic log - tunnel up/down events, IPsec SA negotiation, traffic counters.
Azure Activity log - write operations against resources (create, update). High-value for change tracking and threat detection.
Azure Kubernetes Service control-plane and add-on logs - API server audit, scheduler, controller manager, cluster autoscaler events.
Azure managed disk inventory data - disk SKU, size, encryption state, attached VM.
Azure managed disk snapshot inventory - snapshot source, size, creation time, encryption state.
Azure virtual machine inventory - VM size, OS, power state, attached disks, network interfaces, tags.
Azure Event Hubs ingestion-side logs - producer connections, partition activity, throttling events.
Azure Firewall logs - allowed/denied traffic, application-rule matches, network-rule matches, threat-intel hits, DNS proxy queries.
Azure Front Door access and WAF logs - HTTP request details, applied WAF rules, action (allow/block/log), latency, origin response.
Azure IoT Hub device events - device connect/disconnect, message routing, twin updates, direct method invocations.
Azure Key Vault audit logs - secret/key/certificate access, vault administration, RBAC and access policy changes.
Azure resource platform metrics - per-resource numeric time-series (CPU, memory, network, storage, custom).
Azure Monitor metric stream - unified metric pipeline aggregating per-resource metrics across services.
Azure NSG flow logs delivered in NetFlow format for cross-tool consumption.
Azure NSG flow logs (alternate naming) - per-flow 5-tuple records of traffic evaluated by NSG rules; superseded by VNet flow logs in newer deployments.
Azure Policy compliance results - resource-by-resource evaluation against policy assignments and initiatives.
Microsoft Defender for Cloud (formerly Azure Security Center) alert events - threats detected across Azure, AWS, GCP, and hybrid workloads.
Microsoft Defender for Cloud security tasks - recommended remediations and their lifecycle state (open/in-progress/resolved).
Azure Blob Storage access logs - read/write/delete operations, requester identity, status, latency, bytes transferred.
Azure Virtual Network configuration data - VNet definitions, address spaces, subnets, peering relationships.

description: Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Flow Logs
expanded_desc: Captures detailed telemetry and metadata about ingress and egress IP traffic flowing through your Azure Virtual Networks (VNets) and subnets.
Azure public IP address inventory - allocation, SKU, attached resource, DNS settings.
Azure network interface inventory - NIC name, attached VM, private/public IP assignments, NSG association.
Generic bandwidth metric stream - per-interface throughput data captured by scripted inputs. Performance telemetry only.
Barracuda CloudGen Firewall and WAF log - traffic, IPS, AV, web application security events.
Per-user shell command history (.bash_history) - timestamped command line entries when HISTTIMEFORMAT is set. Forensic source for interactive activity reconstruction.
BeyondTrust Remote Support session log in CEF format - per-session attendee, target, action, and chat transcript metadata. PAM session-monitoring source.
HCL BigFix (formerly IBM) endpoint management log - relevance evaluation, action execution, patch application activity.
ISC BIND DNS server log - query, response, recursion, and error events. Feeds Network_Resolution.DNS when query logging is enabled.
Atlassian Bitbucket access/audit log - repository activity, authentication, admin operations. Feeds Web/Change data models.
Bitdefender GravityZone endpoint log - threat detections, scan tasks, HVI events. Feeds the Malware and Endpoint data models.
Blue Coat / Symantec ProxySG access log - HTTP/HTTPS request URL, category, action, and threat verdict. Feeds the Web data model.
Linux /var/log/boot.log entries - early userspace and service initialization messages captured during boot. Operational diagnostic data.
Box Enterprise event log - file/folder access, sharing, login, admin activity. CASB-grade visibility for the Box platform.
Bro / Zeek log umbrella - conn/dns/http/ssl/files/notice and other per-protocol files. Feeds Network_Traffic, Network_Resolution, Web, IDS data models.

description: Burp Suite web app testing logs
expanded_desc: PortSwigger Burp Suite log - intruder/repeater/scanner activity captured during web application security testing.
VMware Carbon Black (Cb Response/EDR/App Control) logs - endpoint process telemetry and detection events. Feeds Endpoint and Malware data models.
Software House C-CURE 9000 physical access control event - cardholder action, alarm, door, and admin events.
Cloudera Data Platform CLI command output - operational logs from cdpcli interactions with the CDP control plane.
AWS CloudFormation cfn-init helper script log written during EC2 instance bootstrapping - packages installed, services started, files created, commands run from instance metadata.
AWS CloudFormation cfn-wire log - protocol-level HTTP traffic between CloudFormation helper scripts (cfn-init, cfn-hup) and the CloudFormation service. Useful for diagnosing bootstrap failures.
Check Point Quantum Security Gateway log - traffic, IPS, antivirus, anti-bot, threat extraction events delivered via OPSEC LEA or syslog/CEF.
Chef configuration management log - cookbook converge results, resource success/failure on managed nodes. Operational/Change.
Cherwell (now Ivanti Neurons) Service Management incident records - ticket lifecycle and assignment activity.
chkrootkit scan output - host scan for known rootkit signatures and modified system binaries. Feeds the Malware and Change data models.
Repository selection log produced by Red Hat / Fedora subscription manager when a host picks a content repo mirror. Operational only.
Chrony NTP daemon log - time sync source, stratum, offset adjustments. Operational, but critical for SIEM correctness so event timestamps make sense.
CircleCI audit log - account, org, project admin actions, OAuth/SSO authentication, and policy changes from the CircleCI Audit API.
Cisco Application Centric Infrastructure (ACI) fabric logs - APIC controller and leaf/spine switch events, contract policy decisions, endpoint group activity. Operational with security overlap.

Cisco AMP for Endpoints (now Secure Endpoint) telemetry - process execution, file disposition, retrospective convictions, and policy verdicts. Primary EDR source feeding Endpoint and Malware data models.
Cisco ASA / Firepower (in ASA mode) syslog - VPN session, ACL hit, NAT translation, threat-detection, and connection build/teardown messages. Foundational Network_Traffic.All_Traffic source.
Cisco Catalyst switch IOS-XE syslog - link state changes, port-security violations, MAC notifications, AAA decisions. Operational with port-security security overlap.
Cisco DNA Center / Catalyst Center events - assurance findings, network health, client onboarding, and policy push activity for SD-Access fabrics.
Cisco Email Security Appliance (now Secure Email Gateway) mail logs - delivery, spam, AMP file disposition, content filter, DLP, and URL defense actions. Feeds the Email data model.

Cisco Firepower Threat Defense (FTD) eStreamer/syslog - NGFW connection, intrusion (Snort), file/malware, security intelligence, and access control events. Feeds Network_Traffic, IDS, and Malware data models.
Cisco Industrial Ethernet (IE) switch syslog - hardened OT/ICS-environment switching with ruggedized models. Same IOS-class messages as Catalyst plus PRP/HSR redundancy events.
Cisco IOS / IOS-XE classic syslog catch-all - routers and pre-Catalyst switches not covered by family-specific sourcetypes. AAA, interface, CDP, BGP/OSPF and ACL hit messages.
Cisco Meraki cloud-managed log stream - umbrella sourcetype for events from MX firewalls, MR access points, MS switches, and MV cameras delivered via syslog or the dashboard API.
Security-focused subset of Meraki telemetry - IDS alerts, AMP detections, content filtering, and firewall flow rules. Feeds Network_Traffic and IDS data models.
Cisco NetFlow / IPFIX flow records - 5-tuple flow summaries from Cisco routers and switches via the Splunk Stream/Streamfwd or Splunk_TA_flowfix. Primary Network_Traffic source.
Cisco Nexus switch (NX-OS) syslog - data-center switching events: VPC peer state, FEX, FCoE, VLAN/STP, AAA decisions. Operational with security overlap.
Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE) pxGrid session context - user/device session attributes (username, MAC, IP, posture state) published for downstream NAC/SIEM consumers.
Cisco Secure Network Analytics (Stealthwatch) telemetry - behavioral NDR alerts and flow analytics from the SMC. Feeds IDS and Network_Traffic data models.
Cisco Umbrella DNS-layer security logs - per-query category, verdict, identity, and policy. Critical for Network_Resolution.DNS and threat detection.
Cisco Umbrella Secure Web Gateway / SIG proxy logs - HTTP/HTTPS request decisions with categorization, AV verdict, and identity. Feeds the Web data model.
Cisco Web Security Appliance / Secure Web Appliance access logs - on-prem proxy traffic with category, malware verdict, user, and policy. Primary on-prem SWG source for Web data model.
Citrix NetScaler / ADC application delivery controller log - per-request and per-session events when not using the syslog-tagged variant.
Citrix NetScaler / ADC syslog stream - load balancer, traffic, SSL/VPN, and audit events. Feeds Network_Traffic, Web, and Authentication when AAA is enabled.
ClamAV antivirus engine log - scan job activity, signature update events, and detection records on Linux/Unix mail and file servers.
ClamAV scan results - per-scan detections written to scan logs by clamscan/clamd. Feeds the Malware data model.
Direct clamscan command output - one-shot ClamAV scans triggered by cron or scripts. Same finding shape as clamav_scan.
Claroty xDome / CTD asset baseline data - OT asset inventory, protocol fingerprint, vendor/model attribution.
Claroty xDome / CTD security event - OT anomaly, vulnerability, policy violation, and known-threat alerts on ICS networks.
Cloudflare account audit log - dashboard and API actions performed against the Cloudflare control plane. Feeds the Change data model.
Cloudflare DNS query log - authoritative and 1.1.1.1 resolver query records. Feeds Network_Resolution.DNS.
Cloudflare firewall and WAF event log - per-request rule matches with action, ruleset, and triggering criteria. Feeds the Web data model.
Cloudflare Logpush HTTP request log - per-request CDN/HTTP traffic with security and performance fields. Feeds the Web data model.
VMware Aria Cost (CloudHealth) asset inventory data for Azure subscriptions covering VMs, storage accounts, network resources, tags, and cost-attribution metadata.
VMware Aria Cost (CloudHealth) asset inventory data for Google Cloud projects covering compute, storage, network resources, labels, and cost-attribution metadata.
Linux cloud-init agent log on cloud VMs - first-boot configuration, user data execution, module run status. Operational provisioning evidence.

Cloud-init bootstrap output log written during cloud instance initialization (AWS EC2, Azure, GCP, etc.). Captures user-data script execution, network setup, package installation, and module run results.
Generic configuration data sourcetype - free-form config file contents. Inventory/audit evidence.
collectd metric daemon output - host CPU, memory, disk, network, interface counters in collectd's protocol or write_http output. Performance telemetry.
collectd configuration file snapshot - the running collectd.conf content collected as inventory/config evidence.
Generic command execution audit log - captures executed commands on a host through PowerShell logging, audit subsystem, or commercial agents. Feeds the Endpoint.Processes dataset.
Generic config-too_small artifact - the Splunk Universal Forwarder filename-pattern source emitted when a config-like file is too short to typesniff. Investigate input and reroute.
Generic Splunk_TA_nix configuration file monitor sourcetype - emits the contents of any /etc file listed in inputs.conf for compliance evidence.
Atlassian Confluence access/audit log - page view/create/update, admin and permission activity.
HashiCorp Consul service mesh log - service registration, health-check results, ACL token events. Operational service-discovery telemetry.
containerd container runtime events - container/image lifecycle (create, start, stop, remove). Operational core runtime telemetry.
Splunk_TA_nix CPU utilization metric input - per-core utilization sampled at intervals. Pure performance telemetry.
Cribl Stream internal log - pipeline route status, worker process activity, queue and persistent state. Operational diagnostics for the Cribl observability pipeline.
Linux/Unix cron daemon log - scheduled job invocations and completion status. Operational, with limited security value for spotting unauthorized cron entries.

data_models: Compute_Inventory.All_Inventory, Endpoint.Filesystem, Endpoint.Ports, Endpoint.Processes, Endpoint.Registry, Endpoint.Services, Network_Resolution.DNS
expanded_desc: CrowdStrike Falcon device inventory feed - sensor host attributes, OS, last seen, sensor version, platform. Critical for ES Assets/Identities enrichment.
CrowdStrike Falcon Streaming API event stream - detection summaries, audit events, IDP events delivered as JSON. Foundational Falcon source feeding Endpoint, Authentication, and Change data models.
CrowdStrike Falcon detection events extracted from the Event Streams feed - per-detection records with severity, technique, tactic, behavior, and host context. Feeds Alerts and Endpoint data models.
CrowdStrike Falcon incident records - groupings of related detections with assigned analyst, status, and tactic chain. Used for case-management and ES notable correlation.
CrowdStrike threat intelligence indicators - hashes, IPs, domains, URLs with malware family and actor attributions. TIP-grade enrichment data.
CrowdStrike Falcon sensor telemetry events - process, network, file, and authentication events from the agent. Underpins behavioral detections and threat hunting in Falcon.
CrowdStrike Falcon FileVantage file integrity monitoring events - file/registry create, modify, delete on monitored paths. Feeds the Change data model.
CrowdStrike Falcon Intelligence indicator JSON feed - hash/IP/domain IOCs with threat actor and malware metadata. Feeds the CIM Threat Intelligence framework.
CrowdStrike Spotlight vulnerability findings - per-host/per-CVE exposure with remediation guidance. Primary CrowdStrike VMS source feeding the Vulnerabilities data model.

data_models: Alerts.Alerts, Authentication.Authentication, Endpoint.Filesystem, Endpoint.Processes, Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks, Malware.Malware_Attacks
expanded_desc: CrowdStrike unified alerts JSON - normalized alert schema spanning Falcon detections, Identity Protection, mobile, and cloud alerts. Modern preferred ingest path.

description: Splunk default sourcetype for CSV files
expanded_desc: Splunk default sourcetype for CSV files - tabular lookup-style data ingested verbatim. CIM mapping depends on file content; usually used for lookups rather than primary events.
Cisco Telemetry Agent (formerly Tetration / Secure Workload) Linux sensor log - workload telemetry, flow and process visibility. Security-relevant micro-segmentation source.
Cisco Telemetry Agent handler component log - the userland process that ships sensor data, with rotation suffixes typically following the same pattern as the agent.
CUPS print server access log - print job requests with user, printer, document size, and result. Feeds the Web/Inventory data models lightly.
CUPS print server error log - print job failures, driver errors, and daemon diagnostic messages.
CyberArk Conjur log - secret retrieval, policy load/update, and authn events from the open-source / DAP secrets engine.
CyberArk Endpoint Privilege Manager - elevation, application policy verdict, threat detection events from the EPM agent.
CyberArk Privileged Access Manager session and password activity - vault retrieve, PSM session start/end, recording metadata.
CyberArk Vault audit log - core PAM vault events: safe access, password change, account add/remove, admin actions.
Cylance / BlackBerry CylancePROTECT log - AI-driven malware verdict and memory protection events. Feeds the Malware and Endpoint data models.
Datadog event stream - alert state changes, deploys, custom events ingested from the Datadog Events API for cross-tool correlation.
IBM Db2 audit log - SQL access, authorization, security, validation event categories. Feeds the Change data model for Db2 instances.
Delinea (formerly Thycotic) Privilege Manager log - endpoint application elevation, allow/deny policy decisions, justification capture.
Delinea Secret Server audit log - secret access, password change, folder permission, and admin activity. PAM Change/Authentication contributor.
Azure Monitor Dependency Agent installation log on Linux/Windows VMs - install progress, prerequisite checks, registration with the workspace.
Splunk_TA_nix `df` scripted input - filesystem mount, size, used, available, and percentage capacity. Storage capacity monitoring.
Generic DHCP server log catch-all - lease offer/request/ack/release. Critical for IP-to-MAC-to-asset attribution. Feeds the Network_Sessions data model.
Linux/Unix kernel ring buffer output - boot messages, hardware events, driver state changes. Operational diagnostics.
DNF (Dandified YUM) package manager top-level log from /var/log/dnf.log - install/update/remove transactions on RHEL 8+, Fedora, CentOS Stream, Rocky, and Alma.
DNF librepo subcomponent log - records repository metadata download activity (mirrorlist resolution, repodata fetches) underneath the dnf transaction layer.
Same as dnf.librepo - the explicit filename form created by some Splunk_TA_nix inputs that monitor /var/log/dnf.librepo.log directly.
Explicit filename form of dnf - same content as the dnf sourcetype but routed when the input monitors the .log path literally.
DNF/RPM transaction log from /var/log/dnf.rpm.log - low-level rpm operations performed on behalf of dnf. Useful for package inventory and CIM Updates dataset.
Generic DNS server/event sourcetype - should be routed to a vendor-specific sourcetype (bind, infoblox:dns, etc.). Feeds Network_Resolution.DNS.
Docker daemon log - container start/stop/die, image pull, network attach events. Operational with Change overlap when container lifecycle is security-relevant.
Keycloak logs collected from a Dockerized deployment - same authentication/admin event payload as keycloak, namespaced by the container runtime that captured it.
Dovecot IMAP/POP3 server log - login success/failure, mailbox access, and protocol session events.
Debian dpkg package operations log from /var/log/dpkg.log - per-package install/upgrade/remove status, the source of truth beneath apt for Updates data model mapping.
Dragos Platform OT/ICS threat event - asset, vulnerability, and threat behavior detections in industrial environments.
Dropbox Business event log - team activity, sharing, sign-in, and admin events from the Dropbox API. CASB-grade audit trail.
Cisco Duo Security multi-factor authentication log - admin, authentication, and telephony event types from the Duo Admin API. Foundational MFA Authentication source.
Dynatrace event/notification stream - problem events, lifecycle, deployment markers from the Dynatrace API.
Dynatrace problem notifications - opened/closed problem objects with impacted entities and root cause analysis output.
AWS ECS / EKS Container Insights performance log group - CPU, memory, network, and disk metrics per cluster/node/pod. Operational metric stream.
Elasticsearch cluster log - master, gateway, indexing, shard, and slow-log content. Operational platform health.
Generic error log catch-all - vendor varies. Should be re-routed to a parsed sourcetype where possible.
Generic error_log file sourcetype - typically Apache or other web/server error stream when no specific routing exists.
ESET endpoint security log - detection, firewall, web protection events from the ESET PROTECT console. Feeds Malware and Endpoint data models.
Generic command/script execution log - varies by source; often used for scripted-input outputs. Feeds Endpoint data model when context permits.
Exim MTA log - mail accept/reject, routing, delivery, and ACL decisions. Feeds the Email data model.
Azure VM extension handler log - extension install/upgrade/uninstall and command execution events.
F5 BIG-IP umbrella sourcetype - catch-all for older F5 ingest before LTM/ASM/APM subtype routing. Prefer the specific f5:bigip:* sourcetypes.
F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager log - per-user access policy session, SAML/OAuth federation, and SSL VPN events. Feeds Authentication and VPN data models.
F5 BIG-IP Application Security Manager (WAF) log - per-request attack signature, violation, and policy enforcement events. Feeds the Web and IDS data models.
F5 BIG-IP Local Traffic Manager virtual server access log - HTTP request/response with virtual server, pool, member, and HTTP analytics fields.
Fail2ban log - ban/unban actions on services like SSH, Apache, postfix based on failed-attempt thresholds. Feeds the IDS data model.
CrowdStrike Falcon sensor catch-all - typically generated by host-side sensor log files (linux/falcon-sensor.log). Operational/diagnostic agent visibility rather than detections.
CrowdStrike Falcon sensor management utility (falconctl) output - sensor enrollment, configuration, and diagnostic command invocations on Linux/macOS hosts.
CrowdStrike Falcon sensor eBPF subsystem log on Linux - operational diagnostics about kernel telemetry collection via libbpf-based sensor mode.
Red Hat File Access Policy Daemon log - application allowlisting enforcement events on RHEL 8+. Security-relevant for execution control.
fapolicyd access-decision log subset - the allow/deny records used directly for endpoint execution control monitoring.
FireEye/Trellix Endpoint Security (HX/FES) agent events delivered as CEF-formatted syslog messages: malware detections, exploit guard events, and indicator-of-compromise hits.
Fortinet FortiGate Data Loss Prevention log - matched DLP sensor decisions on file/text content with action taken. Feeds the DLP data model.
Fortinet FortiGate DNS filter log - DNS query inspection, blocked domain categorizations, response decisions. Feeds Network_Resolution and Web data models.
Fortinet FortiGate event log - admin login, configuration change, HA, link, daemon health subtypes. Feeds Change and Authentication data models.
Fortinet FortiGate IPS log - intrusion prevention signature hits with severity, attack, action. Primary IDS/IPS data model source for FortiGate.
Fortinet FortiGate traffic log - session-start/session-end records with source/dest IP, port, app, policy, bytes, action. Primary Network_Traffic.All_Traffic source for FortiGate.
Fortinet FortiGate user authentication log subtype of event - portal, SSL VPN web login, captive portal. Authentication data model contributor.
Fortinet FortiGate UTM umbrella log - aggregated webfilter, app control, IPS, AV records when the unified UTM logging mode is used.
Fortinet FortiGate antivirus log - file scan verdicts, blocked malware names, file hashes when AV inspection is enabled. Feeds the Malware data model.
Fortinet FortiGate VPN log - SSL VPN and IPsec session events including dial-up tunnel state and remote user logon. Feeds Authentication and VPN data models.
Fortinet FortiGate web filter log - URL category decisions, blocked sites, override events when web filtering is enabled. Feeds the Web data model.
FireEye (Trellix / Mandiant) NX/EX/CM/HX log - intrusion, email, central management, and HX endpoint detections.
Red Hat firewalld service log - zone/rule changes, panic mode events, and runtime configuration updates layered over nftables/iptables.
Fontconfig cache and configuration data - operational metadata about installed fonts. Minimal monitoring value, rarely indexed in practice.
Forcepoint web security gateway log (Triton / Forcepoint One Web Security) - URL category decisions, malware verdicts, DLP triggers.
ForgeRock Access Management (AM) audit log - authentication, session, authorization, and config events from the AM platform.
Fortinet umbrella sourcetype - typically emitted before subtype routing into fgt_* sourcetypes. Prefer subtype-specific sourcetypes (traffic/event/utm/etc.) for CIM accuracy.
FreeRADIUS server log - Access-Request/Accept/Reject events plus accounting Start/Stop records. Foundational Authentication data model source for wireless and VPN AAA.
Static filesystem mount table /etc/fstab captured as configuration data - useful for CIS benchmark checks and storage inventory.
Generic FTP server log - login, command, and transfer events. Feeds Authentication and Web data models depending on payload.
Assured Workloads regulatory acknowledgment events - records operator approvals of compliance-controlled actions in regulated workload environments. GRC audit trail.

Google Cloud audit logs - admin activity, data access, system event, and policy denied streams covering control-plane operations across all GCP services. Primary CIM Change and Authentication source for GCP.

description: GCP Backup and DR plan details
expanded_desc: Backup and DR Service backup plan metadata - schedule, retention, target resource details emitted via Cloud Logging. Operational inventory of protection coverage.

description: GCP Backup and DR restore jobs
expanded_desc: Backup and DR Service backup and restore job execution events - job state, status, source/target, and outcome. Operational evidence of recovery posture.

description: GCP Backup and DR vault details
expanded_desc: Backup and DR Service backup vault inventory - vault location, immutability/lock state, capacity. Recovery posture and ransomware-resilience evidence.

description: GCP Backup and DR protected resources
expanded_desc: Backup and DR Service protected resource inventory - which VMs/disks/databases are under a backup plan. Coverage reporting source.
Cloud Functions invocation logs - function start/finish, runtime errors, and stdout/stderr output. Serverless operational telemetry.
Cloud Scheduler job execution events - cron-style job triggers, success/failure status, retry attempts. Operational job orchestration telemetry.
Cloud Armor WAF and DDoS protection events - per-request matched rule actions (allow, deny, throttle) on backend services. Feeds Web and IDS (where threat signatures fire).
Same content as gcp:audit, produced by the Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform when reading from a Cloud Logging sink. The colonless variant google:gcp:cloudaudit was the legacy form.
Google Cloud VPC firewall rule logging - per-connection allow/deny decisions on VPC firewall rules with logging enabled. Feeds Network_Traffic.All_Traffic.
Shielded VM integrity monitoring events - boot/launch attestation results, vTPM measurement mismatches indicating possible tampering. Endpoint integrity signal.
VM Manager extension policy events - guest policy assignment, recipe execution, package state on Compute Engine instances. Configuration management telemetry.
Cloud DNS query logs - per-query records emitted by Cloud DNS public and private zones with logging enabled. Feeds Network_Resolution.DNS.
GCP IAM-scoped subset of Cloud Audit Logs - permission grants, role binding changes, service account key creation/deletion. Critical for Authentication and Change data models.
Cloud VPN IPsec tunnel events - tunnel up/down transitions, IKE phase 1/2 errors. Operational state for hybrid connectivity.
GKE Kubernetes API server audit logs - control-plane API calls (kubectl, controllers, service accounts) with verb, resource, user, and authz decision. Primary GKE Change/Authentication source.
GKE Cluster Autoscaler visibility events - scale-up/down decisions, unschedulable pod reasons, node-pool resize activity. Operational autoscaler diagnostics.
Compute Engine planned maintenance events - host maintenance notifications, live-migration markers, scheduled reboot windows. Operational continuity input.

Cloud NAT VPC flow logs - source-NAT translation records for outbound traffic from private VMs/serverless. Provides source IP/port to translated IP/port mapping required to attribute external traffic.
Network Intelligence Center analyzer findings - configuration drift, connectivity issues, and posture problems detected across the customer VPC topology.
Cloud Pub/Sub message and topic operational logs - publish/acknowledge activity, dead-letter routing. Used for ingestion-pipeline observability rather than the message payloads.
Google Cloud Memorystore for Redis operational logs: command execution, errors, slow log entries, and connection events.
Cloud Router events - BGP session state changes and dynamic-route activity for hybrid (Cloud VPN / Interconnect) connections. Operational telemetry.
Security Command Center findings - normalized security/compliance findings from native SCC detectors and integrated partner sources. Feeds Alerts and Vulnerabilities data models.
Cloud Storage usage and access logs - per-request bucket access including operation, principal, response code. Critical for bucket data-exfil and misconfig forensics.
GCP VPC Flow Logs - 5-tuple traffic samples between VM/serverless endpoints inside VPCs. Feeds Network_Traffic.All_Traffic for cloud east-west and egress visibility.
Genetec Security Center event - unified physical security platform records covering access control, video, ALPR, and intrusion.
GitHub umbrella sourcetype - typically pre-dates audit log routing. Prefer github:audit for organizations on GitHub Enterprise.
GitHub Enterprise audit log - organization and repository administrative events, OAuth/PAT activity, permissions changes. Feeds the Change data model.
GitLab umbrella sourcetype - typically pre-dates subtype routing. Prefer gitlab:audit / gitlab:production / gitlab:api:json for specific log streams.
GitLab API request log (api_json.log) - per-API call entries with method, route, status, duration, user. Feeds the Web data model.
GitLab application log (application.log/application_json.log) - app-level event stream covering pushes, merge requests, group/membership changes.
GitLab audit_json log - explicit security audit events: permission changes, project/group settings, two-factor enrollment. Feeds Change data model.
GitLab-bundled nginx access log - HTTP-tier access in front of GitLab. Feeds the Web data model.
GitLab production_json log - Rails production tier log including Rails request log lines. Application diagnostic stream.
GitLab gitlab-shell log - SSH-based git operations (clone, push, pull) handled by gitlab-shell. Feeds the Web data model when ssh activity is tracked.
GitLab fallback sourcetype emitted by some add-on versions when filename does not match a known pattern. Investigate input and reroute to a specific subtype.
GitLab workhorse log - reverse-proxy and slow-request handling tier in front of Rails. Operational diagnostics.

GlobalProtect VPN session and authentication events ingested without the Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (which would tag them pan:globalprotect). Covers user connections, gateway/portal activity, and session lifecycle.
GlusterFS CLI tool log - volume management commands and outputs from the gluster CLI.
Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform internal agent log - add-on health, polling status, and error conditions. Operational diagnostic for the integration itself.
Legacy sourcetype for Google Cloud audit logs produced by older versions of the Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform. Same Admin Activity / Data Access / System Event payloads as gcp:cloudaudit.
Compute Engine guest VM syslog forwarded via the Splunk Add-on for GCP - underlying OS syslog content, typically Linux. CIM mappings follow the guest OS, not GCP.
Legacy GCP VPC Flow Logs sourcetype from earlier Splunk Add-on for GCP versions. Same flow payload as gcp:vpc_flow.
Cloud Asset Inventory metadata snapshots - resource hierarchy, IAM bindings, labels for VMs, buckets, projects, and other assets. Feeds the Inventory data model.
Legacy Cloud Router events sourcetype from older versions of the Splunk Add-on for GCP. Same content as gcp:router_event.
Grafana server log - dashboard query activity, authentication, and admin operations.

Security alerts aggregated through the Microsoft Graph Security API from Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Cloud, Defender for Identity, Microsoft Sentinel, and other connected Microsoft security products.
Anchore Grype container scan finding - per-vulnerability match against a container image's SBOM with severity and fix availability.
Anchore Grype scan summary - per-image roll-up of vulnerability counts and scan metadata.
Google Workspace (G Suite) admin and login activity log - admin, login, drive, calendar, token, mobile, groups, OAuth applications. Feeds Authentication and Change data models.
Azure VM extension handler operational log - generic handler-side events shared across multiple Azure extensions.
HAProxy load balancer log - per-request frontend/backend access entries with backend selection and timing. Feeds the Web data model.
Security hardening audit data - typically output of CIS/STIG benchmark tools or scripted hardening checks. Feeds the Configuration / Vulnerabilities data models.
Generic hardware inventory data - scripted-input snapshots of CPU, memory, disk, BIOS, vendor/model. Feeds the Inventory data model.
Harness CD/Platform audit log - pipeline run, RBAC, secret, and admin activity events from the Harness API.
Hawkey library log (used by dnf/PackageKit) - records depsolving and metadata operations. Operational diagnostic value; minimal security relevance.
Generic shell history catch-all - typically bash/zsh/sh command history. Forensic source for interactive command execution review.
Honeywell Process Manager Data (PMD) historian export - operational process data from Honeywell DCS environments.
Citrix ICA Client log - Citrix Receiver/Workspace session connection, channel negotiation, and error events on the user endpoint.
Microsoft IIS web server access logs - HTTP method, URI, query, status, bytes, client IP, user agent, processing time.
Imperva Cloud WAF event log - per-request decision (allow/block/captcha/challenge), attack signature category, and HTTP context. Feeds the Web data model.
Imperva Database Activity Monitor syslog - SQL query inspection, sensitive table access, and policy violation events.
Imperva on-prem SecureSphere WAF syslog - same per-request decision schema as cloud WAF but from the appliance deployment.
Infoblox NIOS admin audit log - configuration changes, login activity, and DDI object modifications. Feeds Change and Authentication data models.
Infoblox DHCP server log - lease offer, request, ack, decline activity. Critical for IP-to-MAC-to-asset attribution.
Infoblox DNS server query log - per-query name, type, response code, source IP. Foundational Network_Resolution.DNS source.
Infoblox BloxOne Threat Defense / RPZ-based threat events - DNS-layer threat block decisions with category and indicator.
Azure Site Recovery (InMage Scout) driver-level logs on protected workloads - replication driver state, write tracking, sync progress.
Red Hat Insights client log - data collection and remediation activity from the insights-client agent on RHEL hosts.
Generic installation/uninstall activity log - typically OS or application installer trace output.
Sysinternals Sysmon installer log capturing service install, driver load, and configuration apply events on Windows endpoints.
Splunk_TA_nix interface statistics scripted input - per-interface RX/TX bytes, packets, errors. Performance telemetry.
Microsoft Intune MDM logs - device enrollment, compliance status, configuration profile assignment, app deployment.
Splunk_TA_nix `iostat` scripted input - per-device disk I/O statistics (reads, writes, await, util). Performance telemetry.
Generic IPFIX (IP Flow Information Export) flow records - successor to NetFlow v9 with templated fields. Feeds Network_Traffic.All_Traffic.
BSD ipfw firewall log - kernel-level packet filter rule hits on FreeBSD/macOS hosts. Feeds Network_Traffic.All_Traffic.
Linux netfilter iptables firewall log - kernel LOG target output capturing accepted/dropped packets per rule. Feeds Network_Traffic when prefixed appropriately.
Cisco IronPort legacy email security logs - same product line now branded Secure Email Gateway. Mail flow, anti-spam, and AMP file activity. Feeds the Email data model.
Ivanti Service Manager (Neurons / HEAT) ITSM ticket data - incident, change, problem records.
Jamf Pro macOS/iOS MDM log - device enrollment, policy execution, inventory collection, and admin activity.
Red Hat JBoss EAP / WildFly application server log - boot, deployment, transaction, and JCA messages.
Jenkins CI/CD pipeline log - build run results, queue activity, plugin actions, audit-trail entries when Audit Trail plugin is enabled.
Atlassian Jira access/audit log - issue lifecycle, workflow, permission scheme, and admin activity.
Output of `journalctl` invocations - structured journald entries exported to text. Use linux:journal where possible for native field extraction.
Juniper SRX / Junos firewall syslog - session, IDP, AV, antispam, content filtering events. Feeds Network_Traffic, IDS data models.
Kubernetes container stdout/stderr log stream collected from /var/log/containers - per-pod application output. Free-form application data; CIM mapping depends on payload.
Kubernetes Event objects - cluster-level events (pod scheduling, image pull failures, OOM kills, node not ready). Operational with security overlap for pod admission failures.
Kubernetes node-level logs - kubelet, container runtime (containerd/cri-o/docker) messages from worker nodes. Operational core source.
Kubernetes pod-scoped log catch-all - typically reframed container stdout/stderr with pod metadata attached by the collector. Application-payload dependent CIM mapping.
Apache Kafka broker log - controller/replica state, leader election, log compaction, ISR shrink/expand messages. Operational platform health source.
Kaspersky endpoint security log - object detected, scan completed, signature update events from Kaspersky Security Center.
Linux kdump crash dump service log - records kernel panic captures and vmcore generation. Operational diagnostic and reliability data.
Keeper Security event log - vault access, sharing, admin activity, and policy enforcement events from the enterprise Keeper tenant.
Underscore-named alternative for the keeper sourcetype - same Keeper Security enterprise event payload.
Linux kernel facility messages from /var/log/kern.log - hardware events, driver messages, OOM killer, netfilter (iptables) kernel logging.
Elastic Kibana server log - dashboard, server, and saved object activity in the Elastic stack frontend.
SSSD krb_child helper log - per-Kerberos-authentication forked child process activity. Authentication detail.
SSSD krb5_child helper log - alternative name for the Kerberos auth helper used in some sssd versions. Authentication detail.
Generic Kubernetes umbrella sourcetype - usually emitted by older collectors before subtype routing. Prefer k8s:* or kubes:* per-subsystem sourcetypes.
Application container output collected by the Splunk OpenTelemetry / Splunk Connect for Kubernetes - the workload's stdout/stderr stream tagged with Kubernetes metadata.
Splunk Connect for Kubernetes container inventory report - container state, image, restart count snapshots. Inventory data model contributor.
Kubernetes data-plane (worker) logs from Splunk Connect for Kubernetes - kubelet, container runtime, kube-proxy. Operational/diagnostic.
Fluent Bit collector internal log stream - the collection agent's own diagnostic and forwarding status messages.
Kubernetes worker host operating-system logs collected alongside cluster logs - syslog, journald data from the underlying nodes.
Kubernetes performance metric stream from Splunk Connect for Kubernetes - resource usage at node/pod/container granularity. Operational metric data.
Splunk KV Store log (mongod.log subset) - the MongoDB-derived KV store engine messages, replication, and slow-query diagnostics. Operational platform health.
Ivanti (formerly LANDesk) Endpoint Manager log - patching, software distribution, and inventory events.
Splunk_TA_nix `last` command output - login history from /var/log/wtmp. Useful for the Authentication data model on hosts where syslog secure isn't shipped.
Splunk_TA_nix `lastlog` command output - most recent login per local user from /var/log/lastlog. Authentication-relevant snapshot.
LastPass enterprise audit log - vault item activity, sharing, admin and security policy events.
SSSD ldap_child helper log - per-LDAP-authentication forked child process activity. Authentication detail.
Log emitted by an LDAP connection test - often a credentialed bind to verify directory connectivity. Authentication data model contributor.
Lenel OnGuard physical access control system event - badge swipe accept/reject, door forced/held, alarm events.
Splunk_TA_linux_auditd parsed audit events - umbrella for SYSCALL, EXECVE, USER, PATH, SOCKADDR, AVC and other audit record types after extraction.
AppArmor LSM events surfaced through auditd - profile denials and allows. Feeds Change Analysis and Endpoint data models.
SELinux Access Vector Cache denial records from auditd - the primary signal for misconfigured or attempted policy-violating access on RHEL/CentOS.
Audit CWD record - current working directory associated with a parent SYSCALL event. Used to enrich command execution forensics.
Audit EXECVE record - the executed program path and full argv vector for process executions. Critical for Endpoint.Processes and command-line-based threat hunting.
Audit PATH record - filesystem path component of a multi-record SYSCALL event. Provides file targets for open/unlink/rename forensics.
Audit PROCTITLE record - hex-encoded full command line of the executing process, often the most accurate source for argv reconstruction.
SELinux-tagged audit events - parent AVC and policy load records used to monitor SELinux enforcement state and denials.
Audit SOCKADDR record - remote address/port associated with a network syscall (connect/accept/bind). Pairs with SYSCALL for network telemetry.
Audit SYSCALL record - the parent event of most multi-record audit messages. Carries syscall number, args, uid/euid/pid, success/exit status.
Audit USER_* records - USER_LOGIN, USER_AUTH, USER_ACCT, USER_START etc. Authentication-grade events feeding the Authentication data model.
Native systemd-journal sourcetype produced by Splunk_TA_nix journald input - structured per-entry fields (UNIT, _PID, MESSAGE, PRIORITY) preferred over text journalctl output.
Linux systemd-journald log entries (variant naming of linux:journal). Kernel messages, service start/stop events, and application logs collected by journald.
Linux Kerberos client/library logs - krb5 ticket acquisition, KDC interactions, error conditions. Feeds the Authentication data model.
Linux LDAP client activity - sssd/nss_ldap directory queries, bind operations, and error conditions used for centralized identity.
Linux RADIUS client/server logs - typically freeradius - authentication, accounting, and error events feeding the Authentication data model.
Snapshot of SELinux configuration (mode, policy, booleans) collected by the Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux. Used for compliance and CIS benchmark reporting.
SSSD-emitted authentication and directory provider activity surfaced under the Linux umbrella - companion to the sssd_* component sourcetypes.

data_models: Authentication.Authentication, Change.All_Changes, Endpoint.Filesystem, Endpoint.Processes, Endpoint.Services, Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks
expanded_desc: Alias commonly used for the auditd log - same /var/log/audit/audit.log content. Foundational source for the Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux audit content.
Auditd parsed sourcetype used by the Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux - feeds the Change, Authentication, and Endpoint data models from SYSCALL/EXECVE/USER_* record types.
Linux boot log captured by Splunk_TA_nix - dmesg-style early kernel and init messages. Operational diagnostics only.

data_models: Alerts.Alerts, Authentication.Authentication, Change.All_Changes, Endpoint.Services, Network_Traffic.All_Traffic, Performance.OS
expanded_desc: Splunk_TA_nix input for /var/log/messages on Red Hat-family systems. Mixed system messages used by countless Splunk-shipped extractions.
Red Hat /var/log/secure authentication log - SSHD, sudo, su, login, PAM events. Primary Linux Authentication data model source on RHEL-family systems.
Elastic Logstash pipeline log - input/filter/output plugin activity, queue depth, error messages. Operational pipeline telemetry.
Logwatch daily system digest - aggregated summary of authentication, mail, kernel, and service activity for the previous interval.
Output of the `lsof` command captured by Splunk_TA_nix - per-process open file and socket inventory. Useful for forensic snapshots and Network_Sessions data.
Generic /var/log/mail catch-all for Unix MTA activity - similar to maillog with the bare facility name used by some distributions.
Linux mail server log from /var/log/maillog or /var/log/mail.log - sendmail/postfix MTA activity, delivery status, and reject reasons.
MariaDB audit plugin log - per-query connect/query/table activity with username, host, command. Feeds the Change data model with database audit.
McAfee/Trellix endpoint security log - on-access scan, threat detected, exploit prevention activity from ePO-managed agents.
McAfee (Trellix) ePolicy Orchestrator centralized endpoint security management events: threat detections, agent check-ins, policy enforcement, and DAT signature updates from managed endpoints.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint extension handler installation log on Azure VMs - extension agent activity during MDE onboarding.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint installer log - per-host install/upgrade progress and errors.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint installer wrapper log - wrapper script around the MDE installer with environment-prep messages.
Generic measurement data sourcetype - per-monitoring-tool numeric samples. Performance telemetry, no CIM mapping by default.
Generic /var/log/messages catch-all syslog file - mixed facility messages on Red Hat-family hosts before journald-only deployments. CIM mappings depend on actual content.
Rapid7 Metasploit framework log - exploit module execution, session activity, post-exploitation actions in pentesting engagements.
Azure Network Watcher agent monitor log on Linux VMs - agent health, connectivity probe results, NSG rule evaluation traces.
Azure Site Recovery VM snapshot helper shell log on Linux VMs - snapshot pre/post hook execution and shell command results.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (formerly ATP) alert and machine event data via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Defender ATP.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint core process and security event telemetry from the Defender agent on Windows endpoints.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint primary event log - threat detections, scan results, real-time protection events.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint core component log - signature engine activity, scan results, and definition updates.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint core component error log - failures within the core engine subsystem.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint diagnostic log - support/troubleshooting telemetry for the Defender agent.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint diagnostic error log - errors emitted by the diagnostic subsystem.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint enterprise event log - cloud-protection, EDR sensor, and policy events.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint enterprise error log - failures in the cloud-protection and EDR sensor pipelines.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint error log - top-level agent errors not specific to a subcomponent.
Microsoft Defender MPLog - high-detail scan, signature lookup, and behavior monitor trace log used for deep troubleshooting.
Mimecast email security gateway log - rejection, hold, delivery, attachment, and URL protection events. Feeds the Email data model.
MobileIron (now Ivanti EPMM) MDM log - device enrollment, compliance, app distribution, and admin activity.
Generic Splunk modular alert action output catch-all - emitted by any custom alert action that writes back to Splunk. Routed to per-action subtypes (notable, risk, victorops, etc.) when configured.
Tenable.io vulnerability summary alert output - results from the Splunk Add-on for Tenable get_io_vulnerability_summary modular alert action.
Splunk Enterprise Security notable event output - records produced by the createnotable modular alert. Foundational for ES Incident Review and Risk-Based Alerting workflows.
Splunk Enterprise Security risk modular alert output - per-event risk score assignments written to the risk index by the risk-modular alert action. Core RBA input.
VictorOps (Splunk On-Call) alert action output - records the dispatch of pages from Splunk searches to VictorOps. Operational alerting bridge.
MongoDB mongod server log - connection, query, write, replication, and slow query messages. Operational platform health.
MongoDB audit log - authentication, authorization, DDL, replicaset, schema operations. Critical for the Change data model.
MongoDB general server log - the mongod.log content surfaced through the Splunk Add-on for MongoDB. Same scope as mongod with add-on enrichment.
Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) audit logs via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (alternate naming to azure:aad:audit).
Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) sign-in logs via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (alternate naming to azure:aad:signin).
Azure Activity log delivered via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services modular input (alternate naming to azure:activity).
Microsoft Defender for Cloud alerts delivered via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services.
Microsoft Defender alert events via the Splunk Add-on - aggregated alert stream across Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Cloud Apps, Defender for Identity.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint core telemetry stream via the Splunk Add-on - device events backing detections and hunts.
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint enterprise event stream via the Splunk Add-on - org-wide event aggregation.
Microsoft Defender event data via the Splunk Add-on - investigation timeline events tied to alerts.
Office 365 / Microsoft 365 Management Activity API - unified audit log across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, Entra, DLP, and Power Platform.
Office 365 / Microsoft 365 audit log - general workload events (subset of ms:o365:audit:all).
Office 365 / Microsoft 365 security events via the Splunk Add-on - threat-related events from Defender for Office 365.
Microsoft Graph Security API alerts delivered via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security (older naming related to GraphSecurityAlert).
Windows DNS Server logs collected via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory - query, response, and error events from AD-integrated DNS.
AD-integrated DNS zone metadata collected via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory - zone names, transfer settings, replication scope.
Active Directory Netlogon service events - secure channel establishment, trust validation, NTLM pass-through authentication.
Active Directory replication events collected via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory - replication partner status, USN progress, replication failures.
Azure audit data via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services - subscription-level audit events from the activity log.
Azure Event Hub data delivered through the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services - generic event-hub pipeline used for many downstream sourcetypes.
Azure network interface card resource metadata via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services - NIC config, IP bindings, NSG association.
Azure subscription metadata via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services - subscription IDs, names, state, billing scope.
Azure virtual machine resource metadata via the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services - VM size, OS, power state, attached disks/NICs.
Microsoft Exchange Server logs - SMTP message tracking, IIS logs from OWA/EWS, transport rules, and admin audit events depending on log type.
Microsoft SQL Server logs - errorlog (server errors, login events, configuration changes), SQL Server Agent log, and (when enabled) Audit destinations.
MySQL server log - error, general query, slow query content depending on monitored file. Operational and audit-grade depending on logging level.
Nagios monitoring umbrella sourcetype - typically alert/host/service streams combined; prefer the subtype-specific sourcetypes.
Nagios host check result stream - up/down/unreachable state transitions, last-check timing.
Nagios service check result stream - per-service OK/warning/critical/unknown state, performance data, and notifications.
ISC named (BIND) generic sourcetype - same DNS server activity as bind, with the alternative file/process-derived name.
Tenable Nessus plugin metadata - plugin family, CVE refs, CVSS, risk factor, description. Lookup data used to enrich finding events with vulnerability context.
Tenable Nessus scan execution log - scan start/finish, scanner host, target counts, and policy used. Operational visibility for scan job state.
Tenable Nessus per-finding results - host, plugin_id, severity, port, protocol, plugin_output. Primary Vulnerabilities data model source from standalone Nessus.
Tenable Nessus Agent logs - locally installed agent activity, scan plugin execution, link/unlink, and update events from the agent host.
Tenable Nessus messages log - scanner-side operational and error messages distinct from the per-finding scan results. Diagnostic visibility.
Generic NetFlow v5/v9 flow records - 5-tuple session summaries with bytes/packets and start/end timestamps. Feeds Network_Traffic.All_Traffic.
Netskope security alerts - DLP, malware, anomaly, and policy violation detections from the SkopeIT API.
Netskope general event stream - app, page, audit, incident, and network events covering CASB/SWG/ZTNA activity.
Netskope tenant infrastructure events - admin and configuration changes to the Netskope tenant. Feeds the Change data model.
Splunk_TA_nix `netstat` scripted input - active TCP/UDP socket inventory. Feeds Network_Sessions and Network_Traffic for endpoint visibility.
New Relic event data - custom event stream from New Relic Insights for application analytics correlation.
Sonatype Nexus Repository audit log - user, role, repository, and security configuration changes.
Splunk_TA_nix nfsiostat scripted input - NFS client per-mount I/O latency and throughput. Performance telemetry.
NGINX web server umbrella sourcetype - prefer nginx:access / nginx:error for parsed extractions. Feeds the Web data model.
NGINX access log (default combined format) - per-request method, URI, status, bytes, user-agent, referer. Feeds the Web data model.
NGINX error log - module/worker errors, upstream failures, configuration warnings.
Nmap scan results - host discovery, port states, service banners, OS detection. Feeds the Vulnerabilities and Inventory data models depending on scan profile.
HashiCorp Nomad orchestrator log - job submission, allocation placement, task state transitions across the cluster.
Nozomi Networks Guardian / Vantage OT/IoT alert - protocol anomaly, asset anomaly, and threat detection on ICS/OT networks.
Generic NTP daemon (ntpd) log - peer state changes, stratum shifts, offset/drift updates. Operational with SIEM-timeline integrity relevance.
Office 365 / Microsoft 365 logs (generic prefix). Often used before more specific sourcetypes were introduced; covers Management Activity API events.
Microsoft Graph API responses captured by Office 365 integrations - users, groups, mail, files, security alerts depending on endpoint queried.
Office 365 / Microsoft 365 Management Activity API audit events across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, Entra, DLP, and Power Platform workloads.
Okta System Log API v1 events - the predecessor naming convention to OktaIM2:log. Same per-event audit stream of authentication, lifecycle, policy, and admin activity.
Okta Identity Management (IM2) application inventory - registered applications with sign-on policies, settings, and assignment counts. Identity_Management data model contributor.
Okta IM2 application user assignment records - per-user app entitlements and scoped attributes. Critical for access certification and entitlement review.
Okta IM2 group inventory - group definitions and rule-based vs. static membership metadata.
Okta IM2 group membership records - user-to-group mappings used for downstream entitlement enrichment.
Okta IM2 System Log events - the per-event audit stream covering authentication, lifecycle, policy, and admin activity. Primary Okta Authentication/Change source.
Okta IM2 user inventory - user attributes, status, last login, custom profile fields. Feeds Identity_Management data model.
Microsoft OneDrive activity - file access, share, download, delete events typically sourced from the M365 audit log.
OneLogin SSO event log - sign-in, MFA, role, and provisioning events from the OneLogin Events API. Authentication data model contributor.
OpenLDAP slapd access log - bind, search, modify, and ACL evaluation activity. Feeds Authentication and Identity_Management data models.
Inventory of listening TCP/UDP ports on a host - typically ss/netstat-derived. Feeds Network_Sessions and Inventory data models.
OpenSearch cluster log - master, gateway, indexing, slow-log content. Operational platform health for the AWS-led ES fork.
OpenSearch deprecation log - API usage that is deprecated in the current release. Operational diagnostic warning stream.
Red Hat OpenShift Kubernetes API server audit log - K8s control-plane API calls with user, verb, resource, and authz decision. Feeds Change and Authentication data models.
Greenbone OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager scan results - host vulnerability findings with severity and reference. Feeds the Vulnerabilities data model.
OpenVPN community / OpenVPN AS server log - tunnel connect/disconnect, authentication, certificate, and routing events. Feeds Authentication and VPN data models.
Opsgenie incident lifecycle alert - trigger, ack, escalate, close events from the Atlassian Opsgenie API.
Oracle Database alert log / audit log - instance startup/shutdown, ORA-* errors, audit_trail events. Operational with security relevance when unified audit is enabled.
GCP OS Config agent advanced security baseline (ASB) performance telemetry - guest agent metrics related to policy enforcement timing. Diagnostic only.
GCP OS Config guest agent telemetry - operational metrics about policy fetch, package state evaluation, and patch task execution. Diagnostic only.
osquery scheduled query differential output - added/removed rows since the previous interval. Optimized telemetry mode for the same Endpoint/Change content.
osquery scheduled query results - per-row interval-based output from scheduled queries (process_events, file_events, etc.). Feeds Endpoint, Authentication, and Change data models.
osquery scheduled query snapshot output - full result-set capture at each interval rather than diff. Used when complete state matters per interval.
OSSEC HIDS agent/manager log - log analysis, FIM, rootcheck, and active response events. Feeds Change, Authentication, and Endpoint data models.
SSSD p11_child helper log - smart-card / PKCS#11 authentication helper activity. Authentication detail.
Generic package management sourcetype from Splunk_TA_nix package input - captures installed package inventory snapshots for the Updates and Inventory data models.
CUPS print page log - per-page accounting records for completed print jobs.
PagerDuty incident lifecycle events - trigger, acknowledge, escalate, resolve activity from the PagerDuty Events API.
