Palo Alto Cortex XDR / XSIAM alert and incident data - cross-platform detection records and analytics output. Feeds Alerts and Endpoint data models.
Palo Alto Networks NGFW configuration change log (CONFIG subtype) - commit, admin login, and configuration delta events. Primary Change data model source for PAN devices.
Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN log (GLOBALPROTECT subtype) - portal/gateway connect/disconnect, HIP check, tunnel state, and authentication events. Feeds Authentication and VPN data models.
JSON-encoded variant of pan:globalprotect produced when ingesting via the Cortex Data Lake / Strata Logging Service forwarder.
Palo Alto NetFlow / IPFIX flow records exported from PAN-OS - 5-tuple flow summaries used for high-volume traffic analytics. Feeds Network_Traffic.
Palo Alto NGFW system log (SYSTEM subtype) - HA state, link state, daemon health, certificate, and license events. Operational with audit-trail security relevance.
JSON-encoded variant of pan:system produced via Cortex Data Lake / Strata Logging Service forwarding.
Palo Alto NGFW threat log (THREAT subtype) - IPS signature hits, antivirus, anti-spyware, URL filtering, WildFire verdicts, file blocking actions. Primary IDS/IPS data model source.
JSON-encoded variant of pan:threat from Cortex Data Lake / Strata Logging Service - same THREAT schema with NGFW + IPS overlap.
Palo Alto NGFW traffic log (TRAFFIC subtype) - per-session start/end records with App-ID, User-ID, bytes/packets, action. Foundational Network_Traffic source.
JSON-encoded variant of pan:traffic from Cortex Data Lake / Strata Logging Service.
Palo Alto User-ID log (USERID subtype) - IP-to-user mapping updates from agents, captive portal, syslog parsers, and XML API. Enables user enrichment across all PAN logs.
Windows Performance Monitor counters for DFS Replicated Folders - replication backlog, bytes received, conflict resolution counts.
Windows Performance Monitor counters for Network Interfaces - bytes/packets sent and received, errors, queue length per NIC.
Windows Performance Monitor counters for NTDS (AD Directory Service) - LDAP searches, replication metrics, DRA inbound/outbound.
Windows Performance Monitor CPU metrics in metrics-format (PerfmonMk) - per-core utilization, queue length, context switches.
Windows Performance Monitor logical disk metrics - free space, read/write latency, IO operations per second.
Windows Performance Monitor memory metrics - available bytes, committed bytes, page faults, working set.
Windows Performance Monitor network metrics - bytes and packets per second by interface, errors, discards.
Windows Performance Monitor physical disk metrics - disk queue length, read/write latency, IO operations per second per spindle.
Windows Performance Monitor process metrics - per-process CPU, memory, handle count, thread count.
Windows Performance Monitor processor information metrics - per-core frequency, utilization, parking state.
Windows Performance Monitor system metrics - context switches, system calls, processes, threads, uptime.
BSD Packet Filter (pf) log - kernel-level firewall log on OpenBSD/FreeBSD/macOS via pflog. Feeds Network_Traffic.All_Traffic.
Splunk SOAR (formerly Phantom) audit log - user actions, asset changes, playbook executions, role/permission modifications. Primary Change/Authentication source for the SOAR platform.
PingAccess audit log - per-request access decisions and admin activity on the PingAccess proxy enforcer. Feeds Authentication and Web data models.
PingDirectory audit log - LDAP add/modify/delete operations and admin activity. Feeds the Change data model with Identity_Management overlap.
PingFederate audit log - federation transactions, OAuth/SAML token issuance, admin console activity. Authentication and Change data model contributor.
Postfix MTA log - smtpd accept/reject, queue insert/remove, and delivery status events. Feeds the Email data model.
Postfix mail transfer agent (MTA) syslog messages including smtpd connections, queue activity, delivery status, TLS negotiations, and reject/discard events.
PostgreSQL server log - connection, query, error, autovacuum, replication messages. Operational; audit content depends on log_statement settings.
PowerShell execution logs (Splunk Add-on for Microsoft PowerShell) - script execution, host activity, and module use.
PowerShell-collected inventory of installed AppX (UWP) packages on Windows endpoints.
privacyIDEA token authentication server log - per-OTP/FIDO2 assertion, admin token operations, policy decisions. MFA Authentication data source.
Prometheus scraped metric data - the Splunk metric-store representation of Prometheus exposition format input. Operational metric telemetry.
Proofpoint email gateway/TAP log - mail delivery, threat detection (URL/file), and DLP events. Feeds the Email data model.
Splunk_TA_nix network protocol statistics scripted input - per-protocol kernel counters (snmp(7)-style). Operational performance data.
PRTG Network Monitor historical sensor data - time-series export of monitored sensor values from PaesslerPRTG.
Splunk_TA_nix `ps` scripted input - process snapshots (pid, ppid, user, cmdline, %cpu, %mem). Feeds Endpoint.Processes inventory.
Puppet agent log - catalog compile/apply results, resource changes. Operational with Change data model contribution for managed configuration.
Qualys VM scan results - per-host/per-QID findings with severity, CVE, type. Primary Vulnerabilities data model source for Qualys.
Qualys Policy Compliance per-host findings - CIS/PCI/HIPAA control evaluations. Compliance evidence for the GRC use case.
Qualys Web Application Scanning findings - DAST results on web applications. Feeds the Vulnerabilities data model with web-app context.
RabbitMQ broker log - connection, queue, channel, federation, and shovel activity. Operational messaging telemetry.
Generic RADIUS protocol log - Access-Request/Accept/Reject/Challenge messages from any RADIUS-compliant server. Authentication data model contributor.
Rancher Kubernetes management platform audit log - cluster/project RBAC actions, agent activity, admin events on the Rancher control plane.
Rapid7 InsightVM / Nexpose scan results - per-host vulnerability findings with severity, CVE refs, and exposure context.
Redis in-memory data store log - replication, persistence, slowlog, AOF activity. Operational health source.
BMC Remedy ITSM ticket data - incident, change, problem, work-order records from the Remedy AR system.
Red Hat Subscription Manager log - subscription/entitlement, registration, repository activity from subscription-manager.

description: Red Hat Subscription Manager cert logs
expanded_desc: Red Hat Subscription Manager certificate activity log - entitlement cert install, renewal, and removal events.
Red Hat Subscription Manager certd background daemon log - periodic refresh of entitlement and product certs.
Rootkit Hunter (rkhunter) scan log - host scan for rootkit indicators, suspect files, and misconfiguration findings.
Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk historian/analytics log - operational telemetry from Rockwell ICS environments.
RPM package database state captured by Splunk_TA_nix - listing of installed RPM packages on Red Hat-family systems, feeds the Inventory data model.
RSA SecurID Authentication Manager admin audit syslog - console actions, token administration, policy change. PAM/Change data model contributor.
RSA SecurID Authentication Manager runtime syslog - per-authentication request/response, agent activity, token validation. Foundational MFA Authentication source.
RSA SecurID Authentication Manager system syslog - appliance-level OS, service, and component events from the SecurID platform.
Rsyslog impstats module periodic statistics - input/output queue depths, message rates, dropped messages. Diagnostic-grade observability.
Generic rules configuration data - rule export/dump from any policy engine (firewall, SIEM, EDR). Configuration evidence.
SailPoint IdentityIQ access event stream - certifications, approvals, role and entitlement changes. Identity governance audit trail.
SailPoint provisioning audit - downstream account add/modify/delete activity executed by IIQ workflows against managed systems.
SaltStack master/minion log - state apply results, return data from salt commands. Operational/Change input.
Samhain file integrity and rootkit monitoring agent log - filesystem change detection and stealth process checks.
SAP HANA audit log - authentication, authorization, structured/system privilege, and admin actions on HANA databases. Critical Change/Authentication source for SAP HANA.
Microsoft Configuration Manager (SCCM / MECM) logs - client policy, software distribution, hardware/software inventory, compliance evaluation.
Splunk scheduler log (scheduler.log) - lists every scheduled search dispatch, status, and runtime. Critical for ES correlation search and dashboard panel monitoring.
Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows scripted input - logical disk inventory snapshot (drive letter, capacity, free space, filesystem). Feeds the Inventory data model.
Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - inventory of installed software packages from registry uninstall keys. Feeds Inventory and Updates data models.
Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - local group membership inventory. Critical for Identity_Management and privilege-creep monitoring.

description: Scripted input for local user accounts
expanded_desc: Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - local user account inventory and attributes. Identity_Management data model contributor.
Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - NIC inventory: IP, MAC, DNS settings, gateway. Inventory data model contributor.
Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - process snapshot (pid, name, owner, parent, command line). Feeds Endpoint.Processes inventory.
Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - Windows Services inventory (display name, start type, state, account). Feeds the Inventory data model.
Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - OS, hostname, manufacturer, model, BIOS, memory configuration snapshot. Inventory data model contributor.
Variant of GetInstalledSoftware - registry-based installed application inventory used by older add-on builds.
Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - listening TCP/UDP sockets via netstat-style enumeration. Network_Sessions/Inventory contributor.
Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - aggregate network configuration including adapters, routes, and DNS settings. Inventory data model contributor.

description: Splunk scripted input: time sync config
expanded_desc: Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - Windows Time service configuration (NTP source, sync interval). Critical for SIEM clock correctness assurance.

description: Splunk scripted input: time sync status
expanded_desc: Splunk Add-on for Windows scripted input - current Windows Time sync state, offset, last sync. Detects drift impacting investigation timelines.
Generic /var/log/secure sourcetype - same content as linux_secure with the bare filename form used by some inputs.
SELinux policy and enforcement messages outside the audit stream - typically setroubleshoot output and policy load notifications.
Sendmail MTA log - mail from/to, ruleset processing, delivery status. Feeds the Email data model.
SentinelOne Singularity EDR event log - threat, deep visibility process telemetry, agent and policy events. Feeds Endpoint and Malware data models.
Azure VM remote session launcher log - session initialization for Azure Bastion or RDP via Azure agent.
Generic sFlow sampled traffic and interface counter records - packet-sample-based flow telemetry from switches and routers. Feeds Network_Traffic.
Generic SFTP server/session log - secure file transfer activity (typically via OpenSSH sshd-internal-sftp or vsftpd). Authentication data model contributor.
Microsoft SharePoint logs - site activity, document access, permission changes (typically from ULS or M365 audit log).
Siemens MindSphere / Insights Hub IoT/OT cloud data - device telemetry, KPIs, and event streams.
Slack collaboration platform log - via the Splunk Add-on for Slack: audit log events, user activity, channel and file actions.
Generic SNMP poll/walk output sourcetype - vendor-specific OID data depending on collector. Operational performance/inventory telemetry.
Generic SNMP trap/notification sourcetype - device-pushed traps with varbinds. Operational alerting feed.
Snort IDS/IPS alert log - signature ID, classification, source/dest IP, and packet detail. Feeds the IDS data model.
ServiceNow Asset Management (alm_asset) table records collected by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow - financial and lifecycle attributes that complement the CMDB CI inventory.
ServiceNow change_request table records - planned change tickets with type, risk, planned start/end windows. Critical for ES change verification workflows.
ServiceNow change_task table records - tasks under a change_request. Provides execution-level granularity for change-correlation use cases.
ServiceNow CMDB base table snapshot - generic CI record set when more specific cmdb_ci_* tables are not collected. Foundational asset reference data.
ServiceNow CMDB Configuration Item (cmdb_ci) parent table - common attributes for every configuration item. Feeds the Inventory data model.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_app_server records - application server CIs (Tomcat, JBoss, WebLogic etc.). Inventory data model contributor.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_computer records - workstation and server computer CIs. Asset enrichment source for Splunk Enterprise Security Assets and Identities.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_database records - logical database CIs grouping schemas and instances. Inventory data model contributor.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_db_instance records - DB engine instance CIs (Oracle, MSSQL, PostgreSQL instances). Inventory data model contributor.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_infra_service records - infrastructure service CIs that aggregate underlying technical components.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_network_adapter records - NIC CIs with IP, MAC, VLAN bindings. Useful for IP-to-asset correlation in ES.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_server records - physical/virtual server CIs. Primary ES Assets and Identities enrichment source from ServiceNow.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_service records - business service CIs. Maps technical assets to business services for ES prioritization.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_vm_instance records - virtual machine CIs across VMware/Hyper-V/cloud hypervisors. Inventory data model contributor.
ServiceNow cmdb_ci_web_server records - web server CIs (Apache, IIS, nginx). Inventory data model contributor.
ServiceNow cmdb_rel_ci records - parent/child CI relationships. Required to reconstruct application stacks and impact graphs in Splunk.
ServiceNow cmn_location records - physical location hierarchy (site, building, floor, room). Used for geo enrichment of assets.
ServiceNow Event Management (em_event) records - normalized events that drove alerts/incidents in SNOW EM. Useful for closed-loop integration with Splunk ITSI.
ServiceNow incident table records - service-impacting tickets with state, priority, assignment_group, opened/closed timestamps. Used for IT-ops correlation and SOAR ticket sync.
ServiceNow problem table records - root-cause investigations linked to recurring incidents. Operational quality input.
ServiceNow sys_audit table - field-level audit history of changes to records when table auditing is enabled. Primary Change data model source for SNOW.
ServiceNow sys_audit_delete table - record-deletion audit trail capturing what was removed and by whom. High-value Change data model contributor.
ServiceNow sys_choice table records - dropdown list value definitions per table/field. Lookup metadata used to decode coded values in other SNOW sourcetypes.
ServiceNow sys_user table records - user account inventory and attributes. Feeds Identity_Management data model and ES Assets/Identities.
ServiceNow sys_user_group table records - group inventory and parent/child group hierarchy. Identity_Management data model contributor.
ServiceNow sysevent records - internal platform event queue. Operational/diagnostic input typically combined with Workflow and Flow Designer activity.
ServiceNow MID Server / agent syslog transaction events - integration and orchestration activity routed via syslog rather than the REST collector.
SolarWinds Network Configuration Manager change log - per-device config snapshot, diff, and admin push activity. Feeds the Change data model.
SolarWinds Orion NPM events - node up/down, threshold alerts, and admin activity from the Orion platform.
SonarSource SonarQube event log - project, quality gate, scan run, and admin activity from the SonarQube webhook/API.
SonicWall NGFW syslog - traffic, IPS, application control, anti-spyware events. Feeds Network_Traffic, IDS data models.
Sophos Central / Intercept X endpoint event log - malware, exploit, ransomware rollback, web protection events.
Generic Splunk-emitted log catch-all - typically used when finer routing has not been configured. Prefer splunkd, splunkd_access, splunk_python, etc. for specific subsystems.
Splunk certificate expiration monitor output - tracks impending expiry for Splunk server/forwarder TLS certs. Operational hygiene, but security-relevant for inter-tier TLS posture.
Splunk internal introspection - disk usage by indexes, components, and volumes (splunk_disk_objects index). Operational capacity telemetry.
Splunk Python tier log (python.log) - tracebacks and messages from Python-based modular inputs, scripts, search commands, and add-ons.
Splunk introspection resource usage - per-process CPU/memory/IO sampled by the introspection generator. Operational platform health telemetry.
Splunk search audit detail (search.log per dispatch / search_messages.log) - per-search diagnostics including parsing, dispatch, and search-time errors.
Splunk Web access log - the Python-tier splunkweb HTTP server access entries. Largely overlapping with splunkd_ui_access depending on Splunk version.
Splunk Web service log (web_service.log) - splunkweb application service messages including session, error, and route diagnostics.
Core Splunk daemon log from $SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/splunkd.log - indexing, search, replication, license, and component messages. Foundational for monitoring the Splunk platform itself.
Splunk daemon REST/HTTP access log (splunkd_access.log) - per-request logs for the management port (default 8089). Used for Splunk admin/API audit visibility.
Splunk configuration change log (configuration_change.log) - records conf-file modifications applied via splunk reload or REST. Important Change data model source for the Splunk platform itself.
Splunkd stderr output (splunkd_stderr.log) - low-level process/library errors emitted outside the structured splunkd.log channel.
Splunk Web UI access log (splunkd_ui_access.log) - HTTP access entries for the Splunk Web frontend (default 8000), useful for SearchHead user activity tracking.
Squid forward proxy umbrella sourcetype - typically routed to squid:access when access logs are the source. Web data model contributor.
Squid forward-proxy access log entries indexed under a path-style sourcetype (squid/access rather than squid:access). HTTP method, URL, client IP, status, cache hit/miss outcome.
Squid forward proxy access log - per-request URL, action, cache result, content type, MIME. Feeds the Web data model.
Squid forward proxy cache.log entries: cache manager events, errors, startup/shutdown messages, and operational diagnostics. Not user-traffic data.
Generic SSH server/session log catch-all - typically routes to linux_secure/auth on Linux or sshd events on Windows. Feeds the Authentication data model.
Apache mod_ssl access log variant - records of HTTPS-only requests on Apache with SSL-aware fields. Feeds the Web data model.
Apache mod_ssl request log - per-handshake SSL/TLS protocol, cipher, and client certificate detail. Useful for TLS inventory and cipher hygiene reporting.
AWS Systems Manager document execution events - records of Run Command, State Manager association, and Automation document invocations against managed instances.
System Security Services Daemon (SSSD) main log - cache, sudo, NSS, and provider activity. Authentication-relevant on AD/IdM-joined Linux hosts.
SSSD Kerberos Cache Manager log - per-user Kerberos credential cache activity managed by the kcm daemon. Authentication-relevant.
SSSD NSS responder log - Name Service Switch lookups for users/groups served by SSSD. Identity_Management data model contributor.
SSSD PAC responder log - Privilege Attribute Certificate processing for AD-joined hosts. Critical for accurate group membership in authentication events.
SSSD PAM responder log - PAM authentication request handling for SSSD-backed services. Feeds the Authentication data model.
Internal Splunk-generated source assigned to summarized events written via collect/sistats/sitop and tstats summary indexing. Should not be a primary search target.
Generic statistics data sourcetype - varies by source. Performance telemetry, no CIM mapping by default.
Suricata IDS/IPS eve.json output - alerts, flows, http, dns, tls, file events. Feeds IDS, Network_Traffic, Web, Email, and Network_Resolution data models.
Splunk_TA_nix swap utilization metric input - swap free/used over time. Operational performance only.
Generic swap file/state data - capture of swap-related state typically from /proc or scripted input. Performance telemetry.
SAP/Sybase ASE audit trail - per-event database access and admin activity. Feeds the Change data model for the SAP ASE platform.
Symantec Endpoint Protection (SEP) log - virus, IDS, application/device control, behavioral detection events.
Generic syslog catch-all - the default sourcetype for unparsed UDP/TCP 514 traffic. Should typically be re-routed to vendor-specific sourcetypes via props/transforms.
Systemd service manager event logs - unit start/stop/fail, dependency resolution, target transitions. Operational availability monitoring.
Tanium endpoint platform log - sensor query results, action execution, comply/patch findings. Feeds Endpoint and Vulnerabilities data models.
tcpdump packet capture output captured as text or PCAP-derived events - per-packet decode for forensic and protocol analysis.
Microsoft Teams collaboration events - messages, calls, meetings, channel activity, app usage from the M365 audit log.
InfluxData Telegraf agent metric stream - host and application metrics shipped in Telegraf line-protocol/JSON format. Operational metric telemetry.
Generic Telnet daemon/session log - cleartext remote login activity. Authentication data model contributor (and security risk indicator on its own).
Tenable.io asset inventory exported via the Splunk Add-on for Tenable - asset UUID, hostnames, IPs, OS, last seen, sources. Critical for ES Assets/Identities enrichment.
Tenable.io plugin metadata - same conceptual content as nessus:plugin scoped to the io tenant. Used as a lookup to enrich vuln findings.
Tenable.io vulnerability finding records - per-host/per-plugin findings with state (open/fixed/reopened), severity, CVE list. Primary Vulnerabilities data model source for Tenable.io.
Tenable.io vulnerability summary asset record - rolled-up exposure per asset including counts by severity. Useful for high-cardinality dashboarding without per-finding fanout.
Tenable Security Center asset list/group records - one entry per defined asset list or static asset. Inventory/scoping metadata.
Tenable Security Center asset inventory - per-host asset records discovered across configured repositories. Feeds ES Assets/Identities.
Tenable Security Center plugin metadata - plugin ID, family, CVE refs, severity. Lookup data to enrich vuln events.
Plural variant of tenable:sc:plugin used by some add-on versions - same plugin metadata payload.
Tenable Security Center scan policy definitions - template-derived policies with included/excluded plugins. Inventory of scan configuration.
Tenable Security Center repository definitions - logical containers for scan data. Operational metadata used to scope vulnerability data correctly.
Tenable Security Center scan result summaries - per-scan completion metadata distinct from per-vulnerability findings.
Tenable Security Center user account inventory - role, group, last login. Identity_Management data model contributor for the SC platform.
Tenable Security Center vulnerability findings - per-host/per-plugin records with severity, CVE list, plugin_output, first/last discovered. Primary Vulnerabilities data model source for Tenable.sc.
HashiCorp Terraform CLI / Cloud run log - plan/apply output, resource lifecycle actions, drift detection. Infrastructure-as-code change record.
ThreatConnect TIP event records - indicator, group, attribute, and analyst activity from the ThreatConnect platform. Feeds the Threat Intelligence framework.
Generic time-sync data sourcetype - typically `date`/`timedatectl`-derived snapshots. Operational telemetry.
Apache Tomcat application server log - catalina.out and per-app stdout/stderr including stack traces. Feeds the Web data model when access logs are included.
Splunk Universal Forwarder artifact - emitted when a monitored file is below the minimum size for sourcetype detection. Indicates very short logs or rotation tail-ends; review and tune props.conf.
Splunk_TA_nix `top` scripted input - process resource snapshots ranked by CPU/memory. Performance telemetry with some process visibility.
Generic tracking data sourcetype - application-specific tracking/event payloads. CIM mapping depends on contents.
Trend Micro endpoint and gateway security event log - antivirus, web reputation, behavior monitoring, and DLP events from Apex One/Deep Security/Apex Central.
Tripwire file integrity monitoring agent log - file/directory change records: added, removed, modified, attribute change. Feeds the Change data model.
Tripwire Enterprise change manager log - asset configuration change events with rule, severity, baseline comparison. Distinct from open-source tripwire content.
Aqua Trivy scan finding - per-vulnerability/misconfig/secret/IaC issue in a container image, filesystem, or repo. Feeds Vulnerabilities and Configuration data models.
Aqua Trivy scan summary - per-target roll-up of finding counts by severity and category.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /agents endpoint. Operational telemetry for the SC API tier.
Tenable.sc API metrics - performance/health metrics emitted by the SC API client component. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /ldapcheck (LDAP integration test) endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /organization endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /plugins endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /repository endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /scanner endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - performance/health metrics for the SC scanner manager subsystem. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /scanResult endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /scans endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - performance/health metrics for the SC server component. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /softwareUpdate endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /status endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Tenable.sc API metrics - per-call performance/health metrics for the /user endpoint. Operational telemetry.
Wireshark tshark command-line capture output - per-packet decode rendered as text or JSON. Forensic packet analysis input.
Red Hat tuned daemon log - records active tuning profile, applied sysctl/CPU tunings. Operational performance management.
Ubuntu Uncomplicated Firewall log - frontend logging for iptables/nftables on Debian/Ubuntu hosts. Connection allow/block events for the Network_Traffic data model.
Debian/Ubuntu unattended-upgrades daemon log - records automatic security update installation. Useful for patch compliance reporting and the Updates data model.
Companion dpkg-level log for unattended-upgrades - the underlying package operations executed by the automated security update process.
Splunk_TA_nix scripted input - inventory of listening TCP/UDP sockets per host. Critical for attack surface inventory and Network_Sessions data.
Splunk_TA_nix scripted input - installed/enabled services snapshot from systemd or init. Feeds the Inventory data model.
Splunk_TA_nix scripted input - sshd_config snapshot. Used for CIS benchmark and SSH hardening compliance reporting.
Splunk_TA_nix scripted input - available/installed package update inventory across yum/dnf/apt/zypper. Feeds the Updates data model.
Splunk_TA_nix scripted input - host uptime snapshots (boot time, load averages). Operational availability data.
Splunk_TA_nix scripted input - local user account inventory from /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow attributes. Feeds the Inventory and Identity_Management data models.
Splunk_TA_nix scripted input - OS, kernel, and platform version snapshot. Inventory data.
Splunk_TA_nix scripted input - vsftpd FTP server configuration snapshot. Niche compliance/inventory use.
Splunk-internal placeholder when sourcetype detection produced no match - investigate the input and configure props/transforms to assign a proper sourcetype.
Splunk_TA_nix `uptime` command output - load averages and elapsed boot time. Pure availability metric.
Inventory snapshot of accounts with interactive logon rights on a host - typically from a scripted enumeration. Identity_Management data model contributor.
HashiCorp Vault audit log - secrets engine, auth method, and admin path activity with request/response hashing. Primary PAM source for HashiCorp Vault.
Vector observability pipeline internal metric stream - throughput and lag per Vector pipeline stage. Operational instrumentation.
Splunk_TA_nix `vmstat` scripted input - virtual memory, swap, system context-switch, and CPU statistics. Performance telemetry.
VMware ESXi hypervisor syslog - hostd, vmkernel, vpxa, fdm, and other ESXi component messages. Operational core source with audit-trail security relevance.
VMware Horizon VDI logs - connection broker, agent, and security server events including user session connects and authentication.
VMware NSX network virtualization log umbrella - manager, controller, and edge messages from NSX-T / NSX Data Center. Operational with security overlap.
VMware NSX Distributed Firewall log - east-west micro-segmentation firewall hits on the hypervisor data plane. Feeds Network_Traffic.All_Traffic.
VMware vCenter Server log catch-all - VPXD and platform service component messages. Operational core source for VMware control plane.
VMware vCenter event stream - user-visible task and event objects (vMotion, snapshot, login, permission change). Feeds Change and Authentication data models.
VMware vSphere event log produced by the Splunk Add-on for VMware - normalized control-plane events from vCenter / ESXi. Similar payload to vmware:vcenter:events.
VMware Tools guest VGAuth service log on a Windows or Linux guest - guest-to-host authentication broker activity. Operational diagnostic, with weak Auth relevance.
VMware Tools daemon (vmtoolsd) log running as root on a guest - guest-host integration messages: heartbeats, customization, quiesce.

description: Who is logged in and what they're doing
expanded_desc: Splunk_TA_nix `w` command output - active interactive sessions with idle time and active command. Authentication data model contributor.
Azure Linux Agent (waagent) operational log on Linux VMs - provisioning, extension handler invocations, heartbeat, and metadata service interactions.
WatchGuard Firebox NGFW syslog - traffic, IPS, AV, application control events. Feeds Network_Traffic, IDS data models.
Wazuh (OSSEC fork) HIDS event log - same agent capabilities (log analysis, FIM, vulnerability detection, cloud) plus added compliance modules.
Cisco Webex collaboration platform logs - meeting, messaging, and admin activity from Webex Control Hub. Operational with audit-trail security value when admin events are included.
Oracle WebLogic Server log - admin server / managed server events, deployment activity, JTA/JDBC errors.
Legacy Websense (now Forcepoint) web security log - same per-request URL filter and category decision payload from older deployments.
IBM WebSphere Application Server log - SystemOut/SystemErr, FFDC, security, and runtime container messages.
Splunk_TA_nix `who` command output - currently logged-in users from utmp. Authentication snapshot.
Generic Windows Event Log sourcetype - the base sourcetype before channel-specific routing. Usually further refined to WinEventLog:Security, :Application, :System, etc.
Windows Application event log - application crashes, MSI installations, application-emitted events.
Windows Active Directory Domain Services event log on domain controllers - directory service startup, schema changes, replication errors, KDC events.
Windows DNS Server event log - service start/stop, zone load failures, dynamic update issues.
AppLocker EXE and DLL channel - executable and library allowed/audited/blocked decisions based on AppLocker policy.
AppLocker MSI and Script channel - script and installer allowed/audited/blocked decisions.
Windows BITS client operational log - background transfer job creation, progress, completion, and errors.
Windows Code Integrity / WDAC operational log - kernel-mode and user-mode binary integrity verifications, blocked load events.
Microsoft Defender Antivirus operational event log - scan results, threat detections, real-time protection state.
Windows NTLM operational log - NTLM authentication audit events for hunting downgrade attacks and lingering NTLM use.
PowerShell operational log - module load events, pipeline execution details (4103/4104), and provider activity. Key sourcetype for PowerShell threat hunting.

Microsoft Sysinternals Sysmon events from the Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational channel - process creation, network connections, file/registry changes, image loads, DNS queries. High-value endpoint telemetry.
Windows Task Scheduler operational log - task creation, registration, execution, and history events. Used to detect malicious persistence.
Windows RDP connection manager log - RDP listener events, incoming session establishment, connection routing decisions.
Windows Firewall with Advanced Security event log - allowed/dropped connections (when auditing enabled), rule changes, firewall state changes.
Windows WMI activity log - WMI query operations and provider activity. Used to detect WMI-based lateral movement and persistence.
Windows Security event log - logon/logoff, account management, privilege use, object access, policy changes. Foundational sourcetype for Windows authentication and audit detections.
Windows System event log - OS-level events: service control, driver load failures, hardware errors, time service changes.
Windows host monitoring data from the Splunk Universal Forwarder host monitor input - processes, services, network ports, drivers, hardware.
Wireshark capture metadata or output - protocol decode similar to tshark with GUI-derived export formats.
Linux wpa_supplicant 802.1X/WPA authentication daemon logs covering WiFi association, EAP exchanges, and key negotiation events.
Windows Server Update Services logs - update synchronization, approval, download, and client check-in events.
Generic XML-formatted Windows Event Log sourcetype - the base before channel-specific routing. Preferred over WinEventLog for richer field extraction.

description: Sysmon events (XML Windows Event Log Source)
expanded_desc: Microsoft Sysinternals Sysmon events collected via Windows Event Log channel Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational in XML format. High-value endpoint telemetry: process creation, network connections, file/registry changes, image loads, DNS queries.
X.org display server logs from /var/log/Xorg.0.log - GPU driver init, input device probing, mode setting. Operational diagnostic data only.
Palo Alto Cortex XSOAR incident records - case data including playbook progress, indicators, and analyst actions. SOAR audit/operational source.
Yubico YubiKey authentication events - OTP validation, FIDO2/WebAuthn assertions, and OTP server activity. MFA Authentication data model contributor.
Legacy YUM package manager log from /var/log/yum.log - install/update/erase transactions on RHEL 6/7 and older Fedora/CentOS hosts before DNF.
Zabbix server event log - operational engine messages: poller activity, trigger evaluation, escalation, housekeeper status.
Zeek (formerly Bro) per-protocol logs - conn.log, dns.log, ssl.log, http.log, files.log, notice.log etc. Foundational NSM data feeding multiple CIM data models.
Apache ZooKeeper coordination service log - quorum elections, session events, znode activity. Operational dependency for Kafka/HBase/etc.
Zoom video conferencing platform log - meeting participation, recording, admin, and login events from the Zoom Reports/Audit API.
Zscaler Internet Access / Private Access NSS log - per-request URL category, threat verdict, application, and user. Feeds Web and Network_Traffic data models.
openSUSE / SLES zypper package manager log from /var/log/zypp/history - install/update/remove transactions on SUSE-family hosts.
